answers.contents:
西方的文学体系和其文化内核紧密关联，语言本身不是瓶颈， 瓶颈在于思维和认知水平是不是到了能产生共鸣的阶段。

本人亲身经历，小学三年级的时候，学校暑假作业是读一本名著，我被安排读《唐吉可德》。 可想而知，痛不欲生，强忍着耐心读完了，虽然里面每一个字都能看懂，但是完全不理解作者想要表达什么。

此外想要从小说中获得一些指导性的东西，很难。有这时间还不如读读华尔街时报，经济学人（需要一定学术功底）， 纽约客，Times周刊，不光能学习纯正的书面英语表达，还能完善具有实践价值的知识体系。
虽然我总觉得用对YouTube的参与来衡量一个人的英语水平很不规范，但我还是觉得，你能看懂文艺一点的youtuber，那就够了。至于为什么你发现有生词，这很正常啊，如果你的母语不是这门语言总有这里一点不会的，那里一点不会的，何况作家是比较有文化的人呢？现在我看品葱用户发言也有时要查wiktionary或问我的中国同事。
你可以先想一下一个人汉语文到什么水平才能欣赏红楼梦，反正包维尼的识字量肯定是不够的
遇到不懂的单词，首先通过上下文揣测含义，实在不懂再去翻词典。

这些小说你真的感兴趣吗？学英语就是学英语而已，在初期没有必要非去看经济学人之类的学知识。
想要提高，有兴趣看下去才是关键，千万别勉强自己。

我个人比较喜欢看轻松一些的小说。给你推荐Dial A for Aunties 作者:Jesse Q. Sutanto. 非常畅销的小说，网飞都要把它拍成剧了。还有Vera Wong's Unsolicited Advice for Murderers看起来也比较温馨轻松，简单。

这个作者Jesse Q. 父母是中国人，移民到印尼，但是她本身在新加坡长大，母语是英语。她书里面会出现一些中文词，遣词造句比较简单，有人说比较幼稚，但是绝对还是会碰到生词的。生词不多，学起来才更有效率。

对于文学作品而言，读懂（看懂故事情节）不难，但是需要一定的阅读量/英文水平才能读通（感受到语言之美）。

如果本来没有读翻译版文学经典名著的爱好，《双城记》《简爱》之类的，纯纯地只是想提高英文阅读水平，建议还是找一些自己更感兴趣的题材，上面有别的葱油推荐的畅销书就不错。
雅思8分以上至少，算是能读懂英文小说和能去美国电影院看无字幕电影的底线，这些远比在国外上课要难道多，因为有很多生活化的词汇，需要的词汇量巨大，gre单词全部熟悉，20000词汇量差不多。

discussions.content: 过四级以前就可以看双城记简爱了，边看边学，根本不需要gre的水平，gre也不需要20000词汇
其实看电影和看小说还算好, 因为对应的听力和阅读到8分不是完全没可能. 但是对于国人而言, 主要难的是写作和口语这种输出.

answers.contents:
看the temple by H. P. Lovecraft，看能明白多少

在德語類的語言中，文學不是法語系的literature，是buckcraft（bookcraft）

https://www.hplovecraft.com/writings/texts/fiction/te.aspx

wikisource有大量copyright-free的資源，要留意美國的版權法，作者死了多少年後作品進入公有領域
实在不行先读读珀西杰克逊试试，你能无障碍读那个那杰克伦敦也没啥太大难度了
英语词汇和短语太多，多义词太多，生僻词太多。没有20000以上的词汇量，还并不包括短语，一词多义，阅读英文文艺一点的小说可谓是毫无体验。

“It was the best of times, it was the worst of times, it was the age of wisdom, it was the age of foolishness, it was the epoch of belief, it was the epoch of incredulity, it was the season of Light, it was the season of Darkness, it was the spring of hope, it was the winter of despair, we had everything before us, we had nothing before us, we were all going direct to Heaven, we were all going direct the other way”

“I have a dream that one day on the red hills of Georgia, the sons of former slaves and the sons of former slave owners will be able to sit down together at the table of brotherhood.

I have a dream that one day even the state of Mississippi, a state sweltering with the heat of injustice, sweltering with the heat of oppression will be transformed into an oasis of freedom and justice.

I have a dream that my four little children will one day live in a nation where they will not be judged by the color of their skin but by the content of their character. I have a dream today.

I have a dream that one day down in Alabama with its vicious racists, with its governor having his lips dripping with the words of interposition and nullification, one day right down in Alabama little Black boys and Black girls will be able to join hands with little white boys and white girls as sisters and brothers. I have a dream today.

I have a dream that one day every valley shall be exalted, every hill and mountain shall be made low, the rough places will be made plain, and the crooked places will be made straight, and the glory of the Lord shall be revealed, and all flesh shall see it together.”

“Freedom has many difficulties and democracy is not perfect, but we have never had to put a wall up to keep our people in, to prevent them from leaving us. I want to say, on behalf of my countrymen, who live many miles away on the other side of the Atlantic, who are far distant from you, that they take the greatest pride that they have been able to share with you, even from a distance, the story of the last 18 years. I know of no town, no city, that has been besieged for 18 years that still lives with the vitality and the force, and the hope and the determination of the city of West Berlin. While the wall is the most obvious and vivid demonstration of the failures of the Communist system, for all the world to see, we take no satisfaction in it, for it is, as your Mayor has said, an offense not only against history but an offense against humanity, separating families, dividing husbands and wives and brothers and sisters, and dividing a people who wish to be joined together.”


体验一下吧，看看几千词汇是不是能够看懂并体会“无自由之思想，则无优美之文学”

不要上来就读有点年头的名著。不了解时代背景，就很难get到作者的精妙，比如楼上读堂吉诃德的小朋友。另外从19世纪到现在，英语也是有发展变化的，有些表达方式现在用得少了，非母语者就会很头大。

找高质量的当代流行小说，尤其是被改编成影视剧还口碑不错的那种。教父的小说虽然老一点，但问题不大。Fox前些年的法医剧Bones，原著者是Kathy Reichs。Amazon马上要出第二季的Reacher，原著者是Lee Child。都非常简单容易读，get不到的地方可以回去看看影视剧。

这个是非常非常难的，很多英语是母语的人都看不来。
简单说，没有三万词汇量，很难无障碍通读。
三万词汇量要背多久？
一辈子，背到死。
英语就是这么难，比汉语难太多。

It depends on the effect you want and the literary degree of the fiction.


In my situation, I just get the  B1 level English ability in CEFR test roughly , equals about IELTS 5.0 

I read the original English language fiction difficultly, then I abandoned it. Because it’s too many new words to understand the meaning easy.

If you want to understand the meaning of the fiction completely winthout the dictionaries , you need get the CEFR C1 level English ability at least, equals about IELTS 7.5.


But in my opinion, I think the  CEFR B2 level or IELTS 6.5 English ability is enough to understand the rough and main meanings of the fiction .

It depends on the literature degree of the fiction especially. Hemingway's novel is certainly easier to understand than Ulysses or Neuromancer, it is more suitable for to start reading.

You can learn the English language with the assistance of the dictionaries while you reading the fiction.


Then maybe you want to know how to get the CEFR B1/B2 level or IELTS 5.0/6.5 English ability.

My manner is to imitate the content of the native speaker in the real English surroundings like the youtube videos/ twitch streams / reddit comments/twitter information
